title: [Bolt] Create databases Kubernetes Namespace
description: Create the dedicated Kubernetes namespace 'databases' to host database operators.
details: Create a Kubernetes Namespace resource named 'databases' using a YAML manifest.
test_strategy: Verify the 'databases' namespace exists using 'kubectl get ns databases'.

title: [Bolt] Create sigma1 Kubernetes Namespace
description: Create the dedicated Kubernetes namespace 'sigma1' to host application services and their infrastructure components.
details: Create a Kubernetes Namespace resource named 'sigma1' using a YAML manifest.
test_strategy: Verify the 'sigma1' namespace exists using 'kubectl get ns sigma1'.

title: [Bolt] Deploy CloudNative-PG Operator
description: Deploy the CloudNative-PG operator into the 'databases' namespace to manage PostgreSQL instances.
details: Apply the CloudNative-PG operator's Helm chart or YAML manifests to the 'databases' namespace. Ensure the operator pod is running and healthy.
test_strategy: Verify the CloudNative-PG operator pod is running in the 'databases' namespace using 'kubectl get pods -n databases -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloudnative-pg'.

title: [Bolt] Deploy Redis/Valkey Operator
description: Deploy the Redis/Valkey operator into the 'databases' namespace to manage Redis/Valkey instances.
details: Apply the Redis/Valkey operator's Helm chart or YAML manifests to the 'databases' namespace. Ensure the operator pod is running and healthy.
test_strategy: Verify the Redis/Valkey operator pod is running in the 'databases' namespace using 'kubectl get pods -n databases -l app.kubernetes.io/name=valkey-operator'.

title: [Bolt] Provision sigma1-postgres Cluster
description: Provision a single-instance PostgreSQL cluster named 'sigma1-postgres' in the 'sigma1' namespace, managed by CloudNative-PG, including the 'sigma1_user'.
details: Create a 'Cluster' Custom Resource (CR) for 'sigma1-postgres' in the 'sigma1' namespace. Configure it with a 'sigma1' database, 'sigma1_user' as the owner, 50Gi storage, and a single instance for development. PostgreSQL inherently supports multiple schemas, so no special configuration beyond standard cluster setup is required to allow for the creation of 'rms', 'crm', 'finance', 'audit', and 'public' schemas later.
test_strategy: Verify the 'sigma1-postgres' cluster is ready and its primary pod is running in the 'sigma1' namespace using 'kubectl get cluster sigma1-postgres -n sigma1' and 'kubectl get pods -n sigma1 -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=sigma1-postgres'. Connect to the database using 'psql' from within the cluster and confirm 'sigma1' database and 'sigma1_user' exist.

title: [Bolt] Provision sigma1-valkey Redis/Valkey Instance
description: Provision a single-instance Redis/Valkey instance named 'sigma1-valkey' in the 'sigma1' namespace, managed by the Redis/Valkey operator.
details: Create a 'Valkey' Custom Resource (CR) for 'sigma1-valkey' in the 'sigma1' namespace. Configure it for a single instance using Valkey 7.2-alpine.
test_strategy: Verify the 'sigma1-valkey' instance is ready and its pod is running in the 'sigma1' namespace using 'kubectl get valkey sigma1-valkey -n sigma1' and 'kubectl get pods -n sigma1 -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=sigma1-valkey'. Connect to Redis/Valkey from within the cluster using 'redis-cli' and perform a 'PING'.

title: [Bolt] Configure S3/R2 Bucket Access Credentials
description: Set up Kubernetes secrets for S3/R2 storage access credentials in the 'sigma1' namespace.
details: Create a Kubernetes 'Secret' resource named 'sigma1-s3-r2-credentials' in the 'sigma1' namespace containing the necessary S3/R2 access key ID and secret access key. This secret will be referenced by applications for image storage.
test_strategy: Verify the secret 'sigma1-s3-r2-credentials' exists in the 'sigma1' namespace using 'kubectl get secret sigma1-s3-r2-credentials -n sigma1'. Ensure applications can mount and use these credentials to interact with a dummy S3/R2 bucket (e.g., by attempting a 'put' and 'get' operation from a temporary pod).

title: [Bolt] Initialize sigma1-postgres Schemas and User Roles
description: Create the required logical schemas (rms, crm, finance, audit, public) within the 'sigma1' database and grant permissions to 'sigma1_user'.
details: Connect to the 'sigma1-postgres' database using the 'sigma1_user' credentials. Create the following logical schemas within the 'sigma1' database: 'rms', 'crm', 'finance', 'audit', and 'public'. Grant appropriate permissions to 'sigma1_user' on these schemas.
test_strategy: Connect to the 'sigma1-postgres' database using 'psql' and verify the existence of 'rms', 'crm', 'finance', 'audit', and 'public' schemas using '\dn' or 'SELECT schema_name FROM information_schema.schemata WHERE schema_name IN ('rms', 'crm', 'finance', 'audit', 'public');'. Confirm 'sigma1_user' has necessary permissions on these schemas.

title: [Bolt] Create sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap
description: Create a ConfigMap in the 'sigma1' namespace containing connection strings for PostgreSQL and Redis/Valkey.
details: Create a Kubernetes 'ConfigMap' named 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' in the 'sigma1' namespace. Populate it with 'POSTGRES_SIGMA1_POSTGRES_URL' and 'REDIS_SIGMA1_VALKEY_URL' using the internal cluster service names and default ports.
test_strategy: Verify the 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ConfigMap exists in the 'sigma1' namespace using 'kubectl get cm sigma1-infra-endpoints -n sigma1 -o yaml'. Confirm it contains correct and accessible connection URLs for PostgreSQL and Redis/Valkey by attempting to resolve the hostnames from a temporary pod in the 'sigma1' namespace.

title: [Bolt] Implement Basic Network Policies for sigma1
description: Establish basic network policies in the 'sigma1' namespace to allow internal service communication.
details: Create a Kubernetes 'NetworkPolicy' resource in the 'sigma1' namespace that allows ingress traffic from pods within the same 'sigma1' namespace and egress traffic to the 'databases' namespace for PostgreSQL/Redis access. This ensures services within 'sigma1' can communicate with each other and with the database layer.
test_strategy: Deploy two simple test pods in the 'sigma1' namespace and verify they can communicate with each other (e.g., 'ping' or 'curl'). Deploy a test pod in 'sigma1' and verify it can connect to 'sigma1-postgres' and 'sigma1-valkey' services in the 'databases' namespace. Then, attempt communication from a pod in a different namespace to a pod in 'sigma1' and verify it is blocked.

title: [Bolt] Review and Verify Core Infrastructure Provisioning
description: Conduct a comprehensive review and verification of all provisioned core infrastructure components.
details: 1. Confirm 'databases' and 'sigma1' namespaces exist. 2. Verify CloudNative-PG and Redis/Valkey operators are running. 3. Ensure 'sigma1-postgres' Cluster and 'sigma1-valkey' Redis instances are running and accessible. 4. Validate S3/R2 access by attempting to create/read a dummy object using the 'sigma1-s3-r2-credentials' secret. 5. Confirm 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ConfigMap exists and contains correct, accessible connection URLs. 6. Verify basic network policies are active and allow intended internal communication. 7. Confirm 'rms', 'crm', 'finance', 'audit', and 'public' schemas exist in 'sigma1-postgres' and 'sigma1_user' has permissions.
test_strategy: Execute all individual test strategies from subtasks 1-10. Additionally, deploy a simple test application that attempts to connect to PostgreSQL, Redis/Valkey, and S3/R2 using the 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ConfigMap and 'sigma1-s3-r2-credentials' secret, confirming successful connections and basic operations. Verify schema existence and permissions.
